paras.text: Project submit karne ke baad, dono canteen me baithe thak ke chai pee rahe the.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Yaar tumhe kabhi laga hai ki hum dono milke kuch bada karein? Jaise ek YouTube channel jahan English sikhaye jaaye?

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (aankhein chamkate hue) Wow, ye idea to mast hai! Socho — if we started a channel, we would help thousands of students!

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Haan na! But abhi to bas ek sapna hai, hai na?

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Filhaal to haan, isliye hum 'would' use kar rahe hain — kyunki ye real nahi hai abhi, sirf imagine kar rahe hain. Agar ye real hota, hum 'will' use karte, jaise humne Type 1 me kiya tha.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Ohh, matlab jab kuch imaginary ho, sach me possible na ho abhi, tab 'If + past, would' use hota hai?

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Ekdum sahi! Ek aur example suno — 'Agar main teacher hoti, main sabko free me sikhati' → 'If I were a teacher, I would teach everyone for free.' Dekha 'was' nahi, 'were' use hota hai sab subjects ke saath is type me, chahe I/he/she ho.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Achha ye interesting hai — to agar main abhi teacher hota, tumhe kabhi daant nahi padti grammar ki galtiyon pe.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (hasm ke) Sapno me raho tum, reality me main hi tumhari teacher hoon!

quiz.q: Correct the error: 'If I was rich, I will buy a big house.'
quiz.answer: If I were rich, I would buy a big house.
quiz.explain: Type 2 Conditional me 'if' clause me Past Simple (aur 'be' verb ke liye hamesha 'were') aata hai, aur main clause me 'would', 'will' nahi.

quiz.q: Fill (hypothetical/unreal present): If she ___ (know) the answer, she ___ (tell) us.
quiz.explain: Unreal present situation — If + Past Simple, Subject + would + V1.

quiz.q: Type 1 vs Type 2 — Which sentence shows something purely imaginary, not really possible right now? a) If it rains, I will stay home. b) If I had wings, I would fly.
quiz.answer: b) If I had wings, I would fly.
quiz.explain: Pankh hona ek impossible/hypothetical baat hai, isliye Type 2 (If + past, would) fit baithta hai; option a) ek real future possibility hai (Type 1).
